D5 Design L3 Apply Reference

The 60-30-10 Rule

a dominant ground, a supporting secondary, a small accent — the interface proportion that keeps a palette calm

A common UI heuristic splits colour use into roughly 60% dominant (background and large surfaces), 30% secondary (panels, cards), and 10% accent (buttons, highlights). The accent reads as emphasis precisely because it is rare; flood the screen with it and the hierarchy collapses.

Adjust the three shares (they renormalise to 100%). The mock interface and the proportion bar update live. Near 60-30-10 the accent pops; push it past 25% and it stops feeling like an accent at all.
